---
title: "Moments loss function"
author: "Konrad Krämer"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
  pdf: default
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

### Mahalanobis distance loss function

This loss uses the Mahalanobis distance between treatment group centroids:

\begin{equation}
L_{\mathrm{mahal}}
\;=\;
\sum_{\substack{g=1 \\ g \neq \mathrm{UNUSED}}}^{G}
\left(\mu_g - \mu_{\cdot}\right)^{\top} P \left(\mu_g - \mu_{\cdot}\right).
\end{equation}

\begin{equation}
P \;=\; \Sigma^{-1},
\end{equation}

\begin{equation}
\Sigma
=
\frac{1}{N-1}
\sum_{i=1}^{N}
\left(x_i - \mu_{\cdot}\right)
\left(x_i - \mu_{\cdot}\right)^{\top}.
\end{equation}

Properties:

- Accounts for covariance structure
- Scale invariant
- Particularly useful when covariates are correlated
